beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make coffee and breakfast
beats.reasoning: Low energy yesterday (45/100) suggests Shaan needs a calm morning. Starting at 7:15 gives breathing room before work, matching the quiet vibe from yesterday's decompression.

beats.activity: Commute from Magnolia to downtown Seattle office
beats.reasoning: Magnolia to downtown is a cross-town drive (35-40 min in light morning traffic). Arriving by 8:25 gives a normal start time for a Wednesday.

beats.activity: Morning standup, code review, and pair programming
beats.reasoning: Standard Wednesday morning work block. Shaan pairs well and handles code review, so this is productive time before the heat peaks.

beats.activity: Lunch at Pike Place Market, staying indoors to avoid midday heat and smoke
beats.reasoning: Repeating yesterday's lunch spot habit. At 91F with air quality concerns, grabbing food indoors at the market (already at work location) is smarter than venturing out. Slight buffer for crowds.

beats.activity: Afternoon sprint work, debugging, and team meetings
beats.reasoning: Hot afternoon (peak 91F) keeps Shaan at air-conditioned office. Typical Wednesday finishes sprint work and prep meetings before end of day.

beats.activity: Commute from downtown to Gas Works Park neighborhood
beats.reasoning: Downtown to Wallingford is a 20-25 min drive. Shaan heads straight to the free concert venue to catch the evening outdoor event while it's still light and slightly cooler.

beats.activity: Attend free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park, grab food from vendors
beats.reasoning: Big event with crowds and good energy. Shaan's recent days show social interest (World Cup, texting friends). Evening timing avoids peak heat; brings hydration and lighter food due to air quality.

beats.activity: Commute from Gas Works Park back to Magnolia home
beats.reasoning: Wallingford to Magnolia is a 25-30 min drive heading home after the concert. Ends by 20:40 to allow wind-down time before sleep.

beats.activity: Wind down at home: shower, light dinner, texts with friends, tinker on side project
beats.reasoning: Mirrors yesterday's decompression pattern. After an active evening out, Shaan needs quiet time at home with familiar routines (cooking, texting, side project) before bed.
7.5 hours of sleep, ending before 06:30 next morning. Spans midnight visibly on the timeline, allowing full recovery after a moderately active Wednesday.

diary:
Woke up to another scorcher brewing outside. Grabbed my usual coffee routine in Magnolia before heading downtown—the commute felt longer today, probably because I was already thinking about the heat. Work was solid: pair programming in the morning, then a quiet lunch inside Pike Place Market (no way I'm dealing with that smoke and 91 degrees). The afternoon flew by with debugging and meetings.

Left early-ish to catch the free concert at Gas Works. The park was packed, but there's something nice about standing there with a crowd, watching the city skyline and the stage lights coming up as the sun got lower. Grabbed a food vendor taco and just... existed for a bit. Felt good to step away from screens. Got home, showered off the day, tinkered on a side project while texting friends about how good the weather actually was once you got outside. Tired now, the kind of tired that feels earned.
thought_process: I made deliberate choices around the heat and smoke today. Staying indoors at Pike Place during lunch wasn't laziness—it was practical, and honestly, I needed the break from the office. The concert was a spontaneous energy shift; I could've just gone home after work, but the weather report and free event felt like an invitation I shouldn't skip. It changed my mood, pulled me out of the usual grind. By evening I was more creative on my side project because I'd actually disconnected. The day felt balanced because I let myself bend my routine around what the city was offering.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
